pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py logic explicitly require the dashboard to be named I/O Buffering Efficiency.
Task description and verifier.py logic explicitly require these exactly matched 4 graph titles.
Task description explicitly assigns this role.
Task description focuses on ingest servers and dropped frames in video streaming.
export_result.sh saves the final state to this path, and verifier.py reads from it.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real AWS CloudWatch time-series data from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset, which is injected into Graphite via feed_real_data.py. The task description explicitly sets the role of Video Streaming Reliability Engineer in the Media Streaming industry. The required dashboard name I/O Buffering Efficiency and the four specific graph titles exactly match the expected parameters defined in verifier.py. The final output is correctly defined as /tmp/io_buffering_efficiency_dashboard_result.json in both the export and verifier scripts.
